mission.title: Install and Configure Strimzi Kafka Operator
mission.description: Strimzi Kafka Operator allows you to run Apache Kafka on Kubernetes, simplifying the deployment and management of Kafka clusters. It is ideal for users looking to leverage Kafka's capabilities in a cloud-native environment. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Strimzi Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Strimzi Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Strimzi Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Strimzi Kafka Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kafka-namespace

helm install strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka-namespace --version 0.44.0

```

> Creates a new namespace for the Kafka operator.
> Installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Strimzi Kafka Operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kafka-namespace

```

> Lists all pods in the kafka-namespace to verify the operator is running.

steps.title: Create a Kafka cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kafka cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator/0.44.0/examples/kafka/kafka-persistent.yaml -n kafka-namespace

```

> Deploys a Kafka cluster using the provided YAML configuration.

steps.title: Verify the Kafka cluster
steps.description:
This step checks if the Kafka cluster is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka-namespace

```

> Lists all Kafka clusters in the kafka-namespace to verify the cluster is running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Strimzi Kafka Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Strimzi Kafka Operator and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall strimzi --namespace kafka-namespace

kubectl delete namespace kafka-namespace

```

> Uninstalls the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Strimzi Kafka Operator
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up existing configurations, upgrading the operator, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka-namespace -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

helm upgrade strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka-namespace --version 0.44.0

kubectl get pods -n kafka-namespace

```

> Backs up the current Kafka cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the Strimzi Kafka Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the operator pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kafka -n kafka-namespace -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Kafka broker'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kafka broker may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the Kafka broker logs using 'kubectl logs <broker-pod-name> -n kafka-namespace'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kafka cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kafka cluster may not have completed its initialization.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl describe kafka <kafka-cluster-name> -n kafka-namespace' to check the status and events.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources available.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements for Kafka.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Strimzi operator pod is crashing'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the operator configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the operator pod logs using 'kubectl logs <operator-pod-name> -n kafka-namespace'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kafka 3.4.0, improved metrics collection.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 0.43.0, ensure that your Kafka custom resources are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kafka cluster listed with 'kubectl get kafka -n kafka-namespace' and the status should be 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
